gitCommit.subject: WEBDEV-1475: turn off trace+video for nav-sanity (huge file root cause)
gitCommit.body:
WEBDEV-1475: turn off trace+video for nav-sanity (huge file root cause)

The 100+MB trace zips that kept failing the public-mirror push were
all coming from a single source: the nav-pages-sanity test, which
visits 25-75 pages within one test body. With trace recording enabled,
that single test produces one continuous 150-200MB trace zip even on
retain-on-failure mode (any failure of the broader test triggers a
full session capture).

Other specs are short and don't have this problem. Override at the
describe() level for nav-sanity only — set trace + video to 'off'.

Failures in nav-sanity remain debuggable via:
- the [TESTED] / [NOT FOUND] / [REDIRECT] / [BROKEN LINK] stdout markers
- the full-page screenshots written to screenshots/nav-sanity/
- the structured per-failure messages in the playwright HTML report

If a future investigation truly needs a trace of nav-sanity, drop the
test.use(...) line locally and re-run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

errors.message:
Cannot use({ trace }) in a describe group, because it forces a new worker.
Make it top-level in the test file or put in the configuration file.
errors.snippet:
at nav-pages-sanity.spec.ts:105

[0m [90m 103 |[39m   [90m// report. Failures are still debuggable via stdout markers ([BROKEN LINK],[39m
 [90m 104 |[39m   [90m// [NOT FOUND], etc.) and the per-page screenshots under screenshots/nav-sanity/.[39m
[31m[1m>[22m[39m[90m 105 |[39m   test[33m.[39muse({ trace[33m:[39m [32m'off'[39m[33m,[39m video[33m:[39m [32m'off'[39m })[33m;[39m
 [90m     |[39m        [31m[1m^[22m[39m
 [90m 106 |[39m
 [90m 107 |[39m   [36mlet[39m navPaths[33m:[39m string[] [33m=[39m [][33m;[39m
 [90m 108 |[39m[0m
